_comment: Add to society-events realm in auth-service Keycloak. Only the delta is here — users/roles live in auth-service/keycloak/realm.json.

clientScopes.name: payment-api
clientScopes.description: Grants access to the centralized payment gateway. Calling apps must request this scope explicitly.

clients.name: Payment Reconciliation Service
clients.description: Centralized payment gateway. bearerOnly=true means it never issues tokens — it only accepts them.

clients._comment: Add payment-api as an optional scope to every calling app. Example shown for society-frontend.
Add payment-api as an optional scope to the API gateway client.

_how_calling_apps_get_access.step1: Calling app already has a Keycloak client (e.g. society-frontend, event-management-backend)
_how_calling_apps_get_access.step2: Admin assigns payment-api as an optional scope to that client in Keycloak
_how_calling_apps_get_access.step3: When requesting a token, calling app adds: scope=openid profile email roles payment-api
_how_calling_apps_get_access.step4: Keycloak adds  aud: ['payment-service']  to the token
_how_calling_apps_get_access.step5: Payment service verifies aud claim — rejects any token without payment-service in it
